text: Despite the potential harmony suggested between decolonial and decentring ideas—openings for the latter to put into practice concrete evolutionary processes in the international organisations and institutions managed by the former—the fact is that they are not harmonious as concepts are not clarified. While decentring may add to the EU’s capacity to legitimise and accomplish its foreign policy in a more plural manner, the colonial matrix could well continue or even increase, entrenching its dynamics. Therefore, instead of simply aiming for ‘decentring Europe’ —by the time when we already know all its effects and consequences — it is indispensable to work on thinking a decolonial future that enables a full-fledged democratic release of colonial ongoing relationships.
